title: Bread Making Techniques
content:
KNEADING

Fold dough toward you; press away with heel of hand; quarter turn; repeat. 10 minutes first kneading until smooth, elastic, satiny with air blisters just under surface. Second kneading: few minutes for finer grain. Use pastry scraper for soft doughs. With mixer and dough hook: knead until dough cleans sides of bowl, then finish by hand.

PROOFING (RISING)

Temperature: 75-85°F, draft-free. First rise: double in bulk (1-2 hours). Test: fingertip impression remains. Punch down; work edges to center; turn bottom to top. Second rise: almost double again (shorter than first). Final rise in pans: dough springs back slowly when pressed, fills corners of pan.

High Altitude: Dough rises more rapidly; watch carefully and allow only until doubled to prevent overproofing.

SHAPING

Divide dough; shape lightly into mounds; rest covered 10 minutes. Press to rectangle; roll like scroll or fold outer curved segments to center. Compress ends; seal; place seam-down in greased pan. Ensure dough contacts short ends of pan for support while rising. Cover; rise almost double; preheat oven during this time.

BAKING

Test for doneness: loaf shrinks from sides of pan; hollow sound when bottom tapped. Remove immediately from pan; cool on side on wire rack away from drafts. Cool completely before wrapping. Store in covered tin with ventilation holes. Freeze if holding longer than 2-3 days.

Crust Variations:
- Thick crust: Glass, darkened tin, dull aluminum pans
- Hard crust: Pan of warm water in oven; brush with salted water during baking
- Glazed: Brush with egg wash toward end of baking
- Soft: Brush with butter after baking; cover with damp cloth while cooling

High Altitude: Oven temperature 10-15°F higher; baking time similar.
